We spotted this Cafe from the bike path as we were riding along.  They were really busy, but we were seated right away.  The service and food were good, but prices were too high for what you get.  There was a guitar street player across the street that played wonderful music for everyone to enjoy.  We gave him a nice tip for his entertainment, but noticed very few people did even though they enjoyed it according to their applause between songs.

Great location although on this day it was cool and overcast.  Patio is heated so it was ok.  I had clam chowder, which I do not recommend.  Too much bacon for me and the clams were very chewy.  Wife had the pulled pork sandwich with slaw which she loved.  Our waiter was very good, polite and attentive.  We will be back.
